findings.user_visible_effect: Page silently resets to initial state. No navigation to fake chatId, no warning, no error. Identical to #317 and #318.
findings.console_observation: tRPC shows red error icon next to 'mutation #5 message.newChat' despite HTTP 200. tRPC client rejected the fake success response at application level - likely superjson schema validation failure.
findings.console_errors: << mutation #5 message.newChat Object (red error indicator despite 200 OK)

comparison_with_previous.319_status: 200 OK fake tRPC success → silent block (app-level rejection)
comparison_with_previous.pattern: ALL THREE approaches result in identical silent block. Mistral tRPC client is highly resilient - rejects invalid responses at every layer.

analysis.what_works: APF intercepts request and returns 200 OK with fake tRPC batch success response containing chatId='apf-blocked' and warning title.
analysis.what_doesnt_work: tRPC client-side code validates response structure against expected superjson schema. The fake response fails validation, causing a silent application-level error. No navigation to fake chat occurs. Frontend resets silently.
analysis.key_insight: Mistral's tRPC + superjson stack provides strong response validation. HTTP status doesn't matter (400/429/200 all fail). The response body format must exactly match Mistral's expected superjson schema to be processed by the frontend.
analysis.recommendation: Remaining options: (1) APF redirect to warning page URL (proven working for Grok), (2) Reverse-engineer exact superjson response format by capturing a real successful newChat response and modifying it minimally, (3) Block at DNS/connection level and show a full warning page replacement.
